title: Horn Center for Democracy Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026: Fully Funded Regional Leadership Opportunity for Young Democratic Leaders in the Horn of Africa
summary: The Horn Center for Democracy (HCD) has officially opened applications for the first cohort of the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026 , a prestigious regional leadership initiative designed to empower emerging youth leaders across the Horn of Africa. The program seeks to nurture a new generation of democratic leaders by providing intensive leadership training, civic education, mentorship, regional networking opportunities, and collaborative learning experiences. Young people remain one of the greatest forces for democratic transformation across the Horn of Africa. However, despite representing a significant percentage of the population, many young leaders continue to face barriers to meaningful participation in governance, civic engagement, and policy-making. To help bridge this gap, the Horn Center for Democracy is implementing the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program as part of its long-term Youth Development Strategy, aimed at strengthening democratic governance, civic participation, regional cooperation, and youth leadership across the Horn of Africa. Successful applicants will join a select cohort of only 15 emerging youth leaders from across the region. About the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program The Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) is a regional leadership development initiative created to strengthen democratic values, encourage civic participation, and build cross-border cooperation among young leaders. The program combines leadership education with practical democratic engagement, allowing participants to learn directly from democracy practitioners, governance experts, academics, and experienced civic leaders from across Africa. Rather than being a one-time training event, YLDS serves as the beginning of a long-term regional network of democratic leaders who will continue collaborating through mentorship, partnerships, and alumni engagement. The program forms part of HCD s multi-year Youth Development Programme, which focuses on: Digital civic education Leadership development Democratic participation Cross-border cooperation Youth empowerment Regional solidarity Civic innovation Program Location The residential leadership program will be held in: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Program Dates October 11 – October 18, 2026 Participants are expected to attend the entire residential program and all virtual learning activities. What Participants Will Experience The selected cohort will benefit from an intensive and interactive learning experience that combines physical and online engagement. Participants will take part in: A one-week residential leadership training in Addis Ababa Hybrid online learning sessions Interactive workshops Regional dialogue sessions Peer-to-peer learning Collaborative innovation labs Group projects Mentorship opportunities Regional networking Alumni engagement activities The learning model has been carefully designed to encourage collaboration, knowledge sharing, innovation, and long-term partnerships among young democratic leaders across the Horn of Africa. Training Themes Participants will receive practical leadership training covering a broad range of governance and democracy topics, including: Regional Political Dynamics and Democratization Participants will examine the political landscape across the Horn of Africa, democratic transitions, governance systems, and regional cooperation. Leadership and Emotional Intelligence The training will strengthen participants leadership capabilities while developing emotional intelligence, conflict-sensitive leadership, negotiation skills, and responsible decision-making. Youth and Women s Agency Participants will explore the important role of youth and women in advancing democratic governance, civic participation, and inclusive political processes. Digital Advocacy and Civic Action The program will equip participants with practical strategies for using digital platforms to promote civic engagement, public participation, advocacy campaigns, and democratic accountability. Cross-Border Collaboration Participants will learn how to work collaboratively across national boundaries to address shared governance challenges affecting the Horn of Africa. Grassroots Democratic Innovation The program encourages innovative community-based solutions that strengthen democratic participation and social change. Collaborative Project Design Participants will work together to design practical initiatives that address democratic challenges within their communities. Benefits of the Program Selected participants will have the opportunity to: Gain an in-depth understanding of democratic governance across the Horn of Africa. Develop practical leadership and governance skills. Learn directly from regional democracy experts. Network with young leaders from multiple countries. Exchange experiences and best practices. Build long-lasting professional relationships. Develop collaborative regional projects. Receive mentorship beyond the residential training. Join an expanding alumni network of democratic leaders. Strengthen their ability to influence democratic transformation within their communities. Who Should Apply? The Horn Center for Democracy welcomes applications from passionate young leaders committed to advancing democracy, civic participation, and peaceful political engagement. Applicants should: Be citizens of an IGAD Member State. Be between 18 and 35 years old. Demonstrate leadership potential. Support democratic governance and non-violent political engagement. Have experience in: Civic engagement Youth leadership Community organizing Advocacy Policy engagement Social innovation Political participation Activism Be interested in regional collaboration. Be available for all online and in-person activities. Participate in collaborative project development. Take part in program evaluations. Remain active within the alumni network after completion. The Horn Center for Democracy promotes diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity, encouraging applications from qualified young leaders across the region. Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit: A completed online application form. A current CV (maximum two pages). A motivation letter explaining: Why they are interested in the program. Their experience in community organizing or development. Their commitment to democratic leadership. Any additional supporting documents requested in the application. A 3–5 minute YouTube video demonstrating or explaining their relevant work and experience related to the program. Applicants should upload the video to YouTube and submit only the video URL. Important Dates Activity Date Applications Open July 15, 2026 Application Deadline August 5, 2026 Residential Program October 11–18, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit all required documents well before the deadline, as late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Interested applicants are encouraged to visit HERE to learn more about the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program and review the application guidelines. Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about strengthening democracy, promoting civic participation, empowering communities, and collaborating with young leaders across borders, the Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program offers an outstanding opportunity to develop your leadership capacity while building meaningful regional partnerships. Beyond acquiring new knowledge, participants will become part of an influential network of emerging democratic leaders working collectively to shape a more inclusive, peaceful, and democratic future for the Horn of Africa. Apply HERE Conclusion The Horn Center for Democracy Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026 represents a significant opportunity for young leaders committed to democratic governance and regional cooperation. Through intensive leadership development, mentorship, practical learning, and cross-border collaboration, the program equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and professional networks needed to drive meaningful democratic transformation in their communities and across the Horn of Africa. Interested applicants should ensure they complete all application requirements and submit their applications no later than August 5, 2026 , to be considered for this highly competitive regional leadership opportunity. Discover more Global Leadership program opportunities on OFY HERE The post Horn Center for Democracy Youth Leadership for Democracy and Solidarity (YLDS) Program 2026: Fully Funded Regional Leadership Opportunity for Young Democratic Leaders in the Horn of Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
